data.title: ETFs brace for TSLA earnings as some have allocations north of 10% towards the EV maker
data.description:
Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) investors on Wednesday were on the edge of their seats ahead of the Magnificent 7 member's latest quarterly results.

Whether the EV maker tops estimates or comes up short, the effects it has on markets will ripple out to many exchange-traded funds, as more than 300 have stakes in the Elon Musk-led giant.

More specifically, of the 343 ETFs that have positions in TSLA, six of them have heavily concentrated positions of more than 10%. Highlighted below are those six ETFs:

  • ARK Autonomous Technology & Robotics ETF (ARKQ), 12.01% allocation.
  • MicroSectors FANG+ ETN (FNGS), 12.17% allocation.
  • Consumer Discretionary Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLY), 12.45% allocation.
  • Vanguard Consumer Discretionary ETF (VCR), 12.75% allocation.
  • ARK Innovation ETF (ARKK), 13.03% allocation.
  • Nightview Fund NITE (NITE), 13.47% allocation.

Two other exchange-traded funds that will be highly sensitive towards moves in TSLA are the single stock leveraged and inverse ETFs: T-Rex 2X Long Tesla Daily Target ETF (TSLT) and Direxion Daily TSLA Bull 2X Shares (TSLL).

For reference, Tesla’s Q3 earnings report is expected after the closing bell and analyst estimates expect the EV maker to post revenue of $25.7B and EPS of $0.60.

data.title: Stride schools bears on FQ1 results, inflated impact of ESSER funding
data.description:
Stride (NYSE:LRN) shares wiped out 2 weeks’ worth of losses at Wednesday’s open after the company, which develops curriculums for online courses, significantly beat Wall Street’s expectations for FQ1 and set upbeat guidance. In addition, the company’s enrollment during the fiscal first quarter swelled by 18.5% to 222.6K, well above the 200K enrollment the street anticipated.

“Stride's results not only debunked the bear thesis but point to a strength in virtual K-12 demand that is not well appreciated by investors,” Morgan Stanley’s Greg Parrish and Toni Kaplan said in a note to investors on Wednesday.

The results allay any concerns about the end of Elementary and Secondary School Emergency Relief (“ESSER”) funding, which provided $190M for schools to spend until September 30 in emergency relief aid to help recover from the effects of the pandemic.

“The loss of ESSER funding is a headwind to our revenue per enrollment this year,” Stride CFO Donna Blackman said on the company’s earnings call. “However, this is being offset by a positive funding environment,” she added.

The exaggerated influence from ESSER funding on the company’s financial health was the subject of a Fuzzy Bear short report, the release of which caused shares to fall 10%. While Stride (LRN) acknowledges that the loss of funding will be a headwind going forward, its actual revenue impact was less than 3%, an amount CEO James Rhyu calls “immaterial.”

“If it was anything significantly more than that, I think you would have seen a different profile this year than we're giving,” Rhyu said on the earnings call.

“While there are certainly some headwinds to funding from the ESSER cliff in FY25 (~LSD%), it is clearly manageable, and far from the 25% EBITDA headwind some bears have suggested,” assured Parrish and Kaplan, cautioning investors, however, that Stride’s (LRN) history of low-single-digit growth, enrollment volatility, and lack of visibility will make it difficult for the company to achieve 8-12% revenue growth.

Morgan Stanley reaffirmed its Equalweight rating on Stride (LRN) with a 27% bump in its target price to $94.

Shares are up 30.5% on Wednesday.

data.title: Turkish Aerospace targeted in ‘terror’ attack, interior minster says
data.description:
• Turkish Aerospace Industries, a state-run aerospace and defense company, was targeted in a terrorist attack that left a number of dead, Turkey's interior minister said Wednesday.
  • “Unfortunately, we have martyrs and injured people following the attack,” Ali Yerlikaya said on X.
  • It wasn’t clear who was responsible for the attack on company's headquarters near Ankara. Kurdish militants, the Islamic State group and leftist extremists have carried out attacks in the country in the past, the Associated Press reported.
  • Turkish Aerospace, or TUSAS, makes civilian and military aircrafts, unmanned aerial vehicles and other defense industry and space systems.

data.title: Lloyda Banking Group Q3 underlying profit climbs 14% Q/Q as lending increases
data.description:
Lloyds Banking Group (NYSE:LYG) Q3 underlying profit before impairment rose 14% Q/Q and dropped 8% Y/Y, the company said on Wednesday. Net interest income increased Q/Q as lending activity rose.

"We are making good progress on our strategy and remain on track to deliver higher, more sustainable returns," Group CEO Charlie Nunn said. With that, the company reaffirmed its 2024 guidance.

Q3 underlying profit before impairment of £2.03B increased from £1.78B in Q2 and fell from £2.21B in Q3 2023.

Underlying net interest income of £3.23B rose 2% Q/Q from £3.15B and declined 6% Y/Y from £3.44B.

Underlying other income of £1.43B increased 3% Q/Q and 10% Y/Y.

Operating costs of £2.29B compared with £2.30B in the prior quarter and £2.24B a year ago.

Q3 EPS of 1.9 pence increased from 1.7 pence in Q2 and fell from 2.0 pence in last year's Q3.

Lloyds Banking Group (LYG) stock dipped 1.2% in Wednesday morning trading.

Return on tangible equity rose to 15.2% from 13.6% in the previous quarter and 13.9% in the year-ago period.

Underlying loans and advances to customers grew to £457.0B at Sept. 30, 2024, from £452.4B at June 30.

Customer deposits increased to £475.7B at the end of Q3 from £474.7B at the end of Q2, the company said.

Earlier, Lloyds Banking reports Q3 results

data.title: Lithia & Driveway rises after Q3 earnings beat
data.description:
Lithia & Driveway (NYSE:LAD) reported third-quarter earnings that slightly surpassed expectations on Wednesday, though revenue fell short despite showing year-over-year growth. The stock is up 6.7% in morning trading.

The company, part of Lithia Motors, posted an adjusted profit of $8.21 per share, beating analysts' estimates by 61 cents. Revenue for the quarter rose 11% to $9.2 billion but fell short of the anticipated $9.5 billion.

"Our third-quarter performance was strong, showcasing the team's ability to grow our business by leveraging size, scale, and new opportunities while maintaining operational efficiency," said Bryan DeBoer, President and CEO.

The Board also approved a dividend of $0.53 per share for the third quarter of 2024.

Citi says, "Lithia reported mixed 3Q24 results with new & used unit sales below ours/consensus expectations offset with higher overall profitability. From an Internet perspective, LAD reported 12.1M monthly Unique Visitors across all its digital channels, flat sequentially.

LAD ended the quarter with about $1.1 billion in cash, cash equivalents, marketable securities, and available credit.

data.title: Roche CEO says antitrust regulators should block Catalent sale to Novo Foundation
data.description:
The CEO of Swiss pharmaceuticals behemoth Roche (OTCQX:RHHBY) said he believes antitrust regulators should not allow Novo Nordisk Foundation's to purchase Catalent (NYSE:CTLT).

"It's not a problem for us, but it could be a problem for other smaller players, if there is a restriction in how many (contract manufacturers) are available," Roche CEO Thomas Schinecker said on a media call, according to a Reuters report on Wednesday. "From an industry perspective, it would be a wrong decision by authorities."

The Roche CEO comments come as some other industry players, including Eli Lilly (LLY), Novo Nordisk's main competitor in the GLP-1 space, have reportedly come out against the Catalent (CTLT) deal. Last week, several consumer groups said that the Federal Trade Commission should block the planned $16.5 billion deal, arguing that a deal would hurt competition in weigh-loss drugs.

Schinecker later clarified his comments on Roche's results call on Wednesday.

"I was asked around manufacturing for our, specifically the CT388 because the oral molecule is a small molecule," Schinecker said on the results call. "So we have all of the manufacturing capacity that we need. And here we have agreements with CDMOs and, or CMOs and yeah, and we will continue to build up our own manufacturing. Now, I think in general, if companies start buying up CMOs, that will limit the amount of competition that there can be, given that there are also a lot of smaller companies that may not have as much access to CMOs as we have. And that's where my comment came from. And I stand behind that. I think it doesn't make sense from a perspective from antitrust authorities who wants competition."
In February, Catalent said it had agreed to be acquired by Novo Holdings for $63.50 a share. Novo Holdings will then sell three Catalent manufacturing sites to Novo Nordisk (NVO), which should help increase production of the company’s hugely popular weight-loss drug, Wegovy.

data.title: Alto Neuroscience stock crashes amid failed study, Wedbush downgrade
data.description:
Alto Neuroscience (NYSE:ANRO) shares nosedived 66% Wednesday morning, the first trading session after the company announced its psychiatric drug ALTO-100 had failed a key clinical study.

After market close on Tuesday, Alto said that a Phase 2b study of the drug in the treatment of major depressive disorder, or MDD, failed to meet its primary endpoint. The company plans to analyze the full dataset to determine whether to continue developing the product for MDD.

ALTO-100 is also being evaluated in a Phase 2b study as an adjunctive treatment for biopolar depression.

The company said on Tuesday that it expects to have sufficient cash to fund operations into 2027 and through several upcoming clinical readouts.

In a note on Wednesday, Wedbush said it was dropping its rating on the stock to neutral and lowering its price target to $4 from $29 after removing ALTO-100 and net cash from its estimates.

Wedbush said its valuation for the company is now derived solely from Alto’s drug candidate ALTO-101 for the treatment of schizophrenia, with data expected in the second half of 2025.

data.title: CME Group Q3 earnings surpass consensus, setting records across multiple metrics
data.description:
CME Group (NASDAQ:CME) posted Wednesday Q3 earnings that rose past the average analyst estimate as the derivatives exchange recorded fresh "records across volume, revenue, adjusted operating income, adjusted net income and adjusted earnings per share," said Chairman and CEO Terry Duffy.

"Q3 2024 was the best quarter in CME Group history," he said.

Q3 adjusted EPS of $2.68, topping the $2.53 consensus, rose from $2.56 in Q2 and $2.25 in Q3 2023.

Revenue of $1.58B, slightly trailing the $1.59B expected, climbed from $1.53B in the prior quarter and $1.34B a year before.

Despite the strong quarter, (CME) shares slid 1.6% in Wednesday morning trading.

Clearing and transaction fees accelerated to $1.30B from $1.25B in Q2 and $1.09B in the year-earlier period.

Market data and information services revenue was $178.2M, up from $175.0M in Q2 and $167.6M in Q3 of last year.

Total expenses advanced to $560.0M from $531.9M in the previous quarter and $517.6M in Q3 2023.

Average daily volume was a record 28.3M contracts, including non-U.S. ADV, with EMEA up 30% and Asia up 28% from the same period a year ago.

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, Dow slip, yields tick higher as investors await Tesla earnings
data.description:
The major averages slipped on Wednesday, a day after the S&P 500 index posted back-to-back negative sessions for the first time since early September. Investors will now turn their attention to Tesla (TSLA) earnings.

The S&P 500 (SP500) was -0.4%, the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -0.4% and the Dow (DJI) was -0.2%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 3 basis points to 4.25%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 2 basis points to 4.06%.

Wall Street ended in a mixed fashion on Tuesday, with the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite finishing higher.

"The markets are being weighed down by the recent increase in bond rates and declining hope on the direction of the interest rate," Patrick Munnelly, from Tickmill Group, said.

"With less than two weeks to go before the Nov. 5 election, investors are bracing for volatility in the markets," Munnelly added.

Investors will also keep their eyes on Tesla's (TSLA) Q3 earnings report on Wednesday, which will land after the closing bell.

On the economic front, September existing home sales data unexpectedly dipped as prices climb. U.S. existing home sales fell 1.0% M/M in September to 3.84M.

The Fed's Beige Book will be released later in the day.

data.title: Bridgewater Bancshares announces regulatory approvals for acquisition of First Minnetonka City Bank
data.description:
• Bridgewater Bancshares (NASDAQ:BWB), which owns Bridgewater Bank, has received all necessary regulatory approvals to complete its previously announced acquisition of First Minnetonka City Bank.
  • The Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation and the Minnesota Department of Commerce approved the deal.
  • In August, Bridgewater Bank (BWB) announced an agreement to acquire First Minnetonka City Bank in an all-cash transaction.
  • Pursuant to the deal, First Minnetonka City Bank will merge with and into Bridgewater Bank, with the combined organization expected to have around $4.9B in total assets, $4B in deposits, $3.9B in loan and leases, and nine full-service branches across the Twin Cities.
  • The acquisition is expected to close in 4Q 2024.
  • Shares of the Bridgewater Bancshares (BWB) rose 1.3% on Wednesday.
